summary: The test `test_no_truststore_can_install[GitHub]` failed because pip could not download a package from GitHub due to repeated HTTP 502 errors. The test was retried 3 times and failed each time, indicating a transient network issue.
root_cause: GitHub returned repeated 502 errors when the test tried to download https://github.com/pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip, causing pip to exhaust its retries and exit with code 1.

evidence.quote: ERROR: Could not install packages due to an OSError: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='github.com', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip (Caused by ResponseError('too many 502 error responses'))
evidence.reason: This is the direct error message from pip showing that GitHub returned 502 errors repeatedly, causing the network request to fail.
The test was rerun 3 times (shown by 3 RERUN lines), confirming this is a flaky/transient failure rather than a code bug.
Final test failure summary confirming the test failed after all retries.

evidence.quote: 1 failed, 1080 passed, 44 skipped, 21 xfailed, 2 xpassed, 5 warnings, 3 rerun in 451.05s (0:07:31)
evidence.reason: Only 1 test failed out of 1080 passed, and it had 3 reruns, strongly indicating a transient network issue rather than a code defect.

suggested_fix: Mark the test with a network-flaky retry decorator or skip it when GitHub is unreachable. Alternatively, use a local fixture or mock for the package download to avoid depending on external network availability in CI.
